text_blocks.text: Dutch is one of the closest relatives
of both German and English(n 5] and is said
to be roughly in between them.[n 6] Dutch,
like English, has not undergone the High
German consonant shift, does not use
Germanic umlaut as a grammatical marker,
has largely abandoned the use of the
subjunctive, and has levelled much of its
Features shared with German include the
well as the use of modal particles,
final-obstruent devoicing, and a similar word
loans than German but fewer than English.[n
